ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Leander, Bishop.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Leander, Bishop of Hispalis (Seville).

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: With what ardour I am athirst to see thee thou readest in the tables of thine own heart, since thou lovest me exceedingly.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But since I cannot see thee, separated as thou art from me by long tracts of country, I have done what charity towards thee dictated, namely to transmit to thy Holiness, on the arrival here of our common son Probinus the presbyter, the book of Pastoral Rule, which I wrote at the commencement of my episcopate, and the books which thou knewest I had already composed on the exposition of the blessed Job.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Some sheets indeed of the third and fourth parts of that work I have not sent to thy Charity, having already given those sheets only of the said parts to monasteries.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: These, then, which I send let thy Holiness earnestly peruse, and more earnestly deplore my sins, lest it be to my more serious blame that I am seen as it were to know what I omit to do.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But with how great tumults of business I am oppressed in this Church the very brevity of my epistle will signify to thy Charity, seeing that I say so little to him whom more than all I love.
